author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

title: John 5.39,40

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Search the Scriptures; for in them ye think ye have eternal life; and they are they which testify of Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And ye will not come to Me that ye might have [eternal ] life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Beloved, let us make great account of spiritual things, and not think that it is sufficient for us to salvation to pursue them anyhow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if in things of this life a man can gain no great profit if he conduct them in an indifferent and chance way, much more will this be the case in spiritual things, since these require yet greater attention.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Christ when He referred the Jews to the Scriptures, sent them not to a mere reading, but a careful and considerate search; for He said not, “Read the Scriptures,” but, “Search the Scriptures.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since the sayings relating to Him required great attention, (for they had been concealed from the beginning for the advantage of the men of that time,) He biddeth them now dig down with care that they might be able to discover what lay in the depth below.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These sayings were not on the surface, nor were they cast forth to open view, but lay like some treasure hidden very deep.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now he that searcheth for hidden things, except he seek them with care and toil, will never find the object of his search.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For which cause He said, “Search the Scriptures, because in them ye think ye have eternal life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “Ye have,” but “ye think,” showing that they gained from them nothing great or high, expecting as they did to be saved by the mere reading, without the addi tion of faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith therefore is of this kind: “Do ye not admire the Scriptures, do ye not think that they are the causes of all life?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By these I confirm My claims now, for they are they which testify of Me, yet ye will not come to Me that ye may have eternal life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was thus with good reason that He said, “ye think,” because they would not obey, but merely prided themselves on the bare reading.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then lest owing to His very tender care He should incur among them the suspicion of vainglory, and because He desired to be believed by them, should be deemed to be seeking His own; (for He reminded them of the words of John, and of the witness of God, and of His own works, and said all He could to draw them to Him, and promised them “life”;) since, I say, it was likely that many would suspect that He spake these things from a desire of glory, hear what He saith:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I receive not honor from men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “I need it not”: “My nature,” He saith, “is not of such a kind as to need the honor which is from men, for if the sun can receive no addition from the light of a candle, much farther am I from needing the honor which is from men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then,” asks some one, “sayest thou these things, if thou needest it not?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That ye may be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He positively asserted above, and the same He implied here also, by saying, “that ye might have life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, He putteth another reason:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But I know you that ye have not the love of God in you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when under pretense of loving God they persecuted Him because He made Himself equal with God, and He knew that they would not believe Him, lest any one should ask, “why speakest thou these words?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I speak them,” He saith, “to convict you of this, that it is not for the love of God that ye persecute Me, if it be so that He testifieth to Me both by works and by the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as before this when ye deemed Me an enemy of God ye drove Me away, so now, since I have declared these things, ye ought to have hastened to Me, if ye had really loved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But ye love Him not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore have I spoken these words, to show that you are possessed with excessive pride, that you are vainly boasting and shading over your own enviousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the same He proveth not by these things only, but by those that should come to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am come in My Father’s name, and ye receive Me not; if another shall come in his own name, him will ye receive.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that He everywhere declareth that He hath been “sent,” that judgment hath been committed to Him by the Father, that He can do nothing of Himself, in order that He may cut off all excuse for their unfairness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But who is it that He here saith shall come “in his own name”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He alludeth here to Antichrist, and putteth an incontrovertible proof of their unfairness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For if as loving God ye persecute Me, much more ought this to have taken place in the case of Antichrist.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he will neither say that he is sent by the Father, nor that he cometh according to his will, but in everything contrariwise, seizing like a tyrant what belongeth not to him, and asserting that he is the very God over all, as Paul saith, ‘Exalting himself above all that is called God, or that is worshiped, showing himself that he is God.’ ( 2 Thess. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is to ‘come in his own name.’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I do not so, but am come in the Name of My Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That they received not One who said that He was sent of God, was a sufficient proof that they loved not God; but now from the contrary of this fact, from their being about to receive Antichrist, He showeth their shamelessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when they received not One who asserteth that He was sent by God, and are about to worship one who knoweth Him not, and who saith that he is God over all, it is clear that their persecution proceeded from malice and from hating God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He putteth two reasons for His words; and first the kinder one, “That ye may be saved”; and, “That ye may have life”: and when they would have mocked at Him, He putteth the other which was more striking, showing that even although His hearers should not believe, yet that God was wont always to do His own works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now Paul speaking concerning Antichrist said prophetically, that “God shall send them strong delusion,—that they all might be judged who believed not the truth, but had pleasure in unrighteousness.” ( 2 Thess. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ said not, “He shall come”; but, “if He come,” from tenderness for His hearers; and because all their obstinacy was not yet complete.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He was silent as to the reason of His coming; but Paul, for those who can understand, has particularly alluded to it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is he who taketh away all excuse from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ then putteth also the cause of their unbelief, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How can ye believe, which receive honor one of another, and seek not the honor that cometh from God only?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hence again He showeth that they looked not to the things of God, but that under this pretense they desired to gratify private feeling, and were so far from doing this on account of His glory, that they preferred honor from men to that which cometh from Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then were they likely to entertain such hostility towards Him for a kind of honor which they so despised, as to prefer to it the honor which cometh from men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having told them that they had not the love of God, and having proved it by what was doing in His case, and by what should be in the case of Antichrist, and having demonstrated that they were deprived of all excuse, He next bringeth Moses to be their accuser, going on to say,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father; there is one that accuseth you, even Moses, in whom ye trust.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had ye believed Moses, ye would have believed Me; for he wrote of Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if ye believe not his writings, how shall ye believe My words?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith is of this kind: “It is Moses who has been insulted more than I by your conduct towards Me, for ye have disbelieved him rather than Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See how in every way He hath cast them out from all excuse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye said that ye loved God when ye persecuted Me; I have shown that ye did so from hatred of Him: ye say that I break the Sabbath and annul the Law; I have rid Me of this slander also: ye maintain that ye believe in Moses by what ye dare to do against Me; I on the contrary show that this is most to disbelieve in Moses; for so far am I from opposing the Law, that he who shall accuse you is none other than the man who gave you the Law.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then He said of the Scriptures, in which “ye think ye have eternal life,” so of Moses also He saith, “in whom ye trust”; everywhere conquering them by their own weapons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And whence,” saith some one, “is it clear that Moses will accuse us, and that thou art not a boaster?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What hast thou to do with Moses?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast broken the Sabbath which he ordained that we should keep; how then should he accuse us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And how doth it appear that we shall believe on another who cometh in his own name?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All these assertions thou makest without evidence.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now in truth all these points are proved above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For” (Christ would reply) “since it is acknowledged that I came from God, both by the works, by the voice of John, and by the testimony of the Father, it is evident that Moses will accuse the Jews.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what saith he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If a man come doing miracles and leading you to God, and truly foretelling things future, ye must hearken unto him with all readiness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now Christ had done all this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He wrought miracles in very truth, He drew all men to God, and (so that He) caused accomplishment to follow His predictions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But whence doth it appear that they will believe another?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From their hating Christ, since they who turn aside from Him who cometh according to the will of God will, it is quite plain, receive the enemy of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And marvel not if He now putteth forward Moses, although He said, “I receive not witness from man,” for He referreth them not to Moses, but to the Scriptures of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: However, since the Scriptures terrified them less, He bringeth round His discourse to the very person (of Moses), setting over against them their Lawgiver as their accuser, thus rendering the terror more impressive; and each of their assertions He refuteth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe: they said that they persecuted Him through love for God, He showeth that they did so through hating God; they said that they held fast to Moses, He showeth that they acted thus because they believed not Moses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had they been zealous for the law, they ought to have received Him who fulfilled it; if they loved God they ought to have believed One who drew them to Him, if they believed Moses they ought to have done homage to One of whom Moses prophesied.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But” (saith Christ) “if Moses is disbelieved before My coming, it is nothing unlikely that I, who am heralded by him, should be driven away by you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As then He had shown from their conduct towards Himself that they who admired John (really) despised him, so now He showeth that they who thought that they believed Moses, believed him not, and turneth back on their own head all that they thought to put forward in their own behalf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “So far,” He saith, “am I from drawing you away from the Law, that I call your Lawgiver himself to be your accuser.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That the Scriptures testified of Him He declared, but where they testify He added not; desiring to inspire them with greater awe, and to prompt them to search, and to reduce them to the necessity of questioning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had He told them readily and without their questioning, they would have rejected the testimony; but now, if they gave any heed to His words, they needed first of all to ask, and learn from Him what that testimony was.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He dealeth the more largely in assertions and threats, not in proofs only, that even so He may bring them over by fear of what He saith; but they even so were silent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such a thing is wickedness; whatsoever a man say or do it is not stirred to move, but remaineth keeping its peculiar venom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore we must cast out all wickedness from our souls, and never more contrive any deceit; for, saith one, “To the perverse God sendeth crooked paths” ( Prov. xxi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 8 , LXX.); and, “The holy spirit of discipline will flee deceit, and remove from thoughts that are without understanding.” ( Wisd. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For nothing maketh men so foolish as wickedness; since when a man is treacherous, unfair, ungrateful, (these are different forms of wickedness,) when without having been wronged he grieves another, when he weaves deceits, how shall he not exhibit an example of excessive folly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, nothing maketh men so wise as virtue; it rendereth them thankful and fair-minded, merciful, mild, gentle, and candid; it is wont to be the mother of all other blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is more understanding than one so disposed? for virtue is the very spring and root of prudence, just as all wickedness hath its beginning in folly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, the insolent man and the angry become the prey of their respective passions from lack of wisdom; on which account the prophet said, “There is no soundness in my flesh: my wounds stink and are corrupt because of my foolishness” ( Ps. xxxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3, 4 ): showing that all sin hath its beginning in folly: and so the virtuous man who hath the fear of God is more understanding than any; wherefore a wise man hath said, “The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.” ( Prov. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If then to fear God is to have wisdom, and the wicked man hath not that fear, he is deprived of that which is wisdom indeed;—and deprived of that which is wisdom indeed, he is more foolish than any.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet many admire the wicked as being able to do injustice and harm, not knowing that they ought to deem them wretched above all men, who thinking to injure others thrust the sword against themselves;—an act of extremest folly, that a man should strike himself and not even know that he doth so, but should think that he is injuring another while he is killing himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul, knowing that we slay ourselves when we smite others, saith, “Why do ye not rather take wrong?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why do ye not rather suffer yourselves to be defrauded?” ( 1 Cor. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the not suffering wrong consists in doing none, as also the not being ill-used in not using others ill; though this assertion may seem a riddle to the many, and to those who will not learn true wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Knowing this, let us not call wretched or lament for those who suffer injury or insult, but for such who inflict these things; these are they who have been most injured, who have made God to be at war with them, and have opened the mouths of ten thousand accusers, who are getting an evil reputation in the present life, and drawing down on themselves severe punishment in the life to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: While those who have been wronged by them, and have nobly borne it all, have God favorable to them, and all to condone with, and praise, and entertain them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such as these in the present life, shall enjoy an exceeding good report, as affording the strongest example of true wisdom, and in the life to come shall share the good things everlasting; to which may we all attain through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, now and ever, and world without end.
